item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was missing the

« SCP-1546 | SCP-1547 | SCP-1548 »

I added it, I hope that was okay.

item.title: This is bad
item.content:encoded: Something tells me Director Jethro is only written in as an excuse for the last long note, which is in turn just a lazy way to explain a badly written skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the imagery here…And the issue with containment attempts driving 1547 to manifest in increasingly densely populated areas. I agree that the end note is long, but I for one appreciate the detail here. Great work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey! I see this has been rewritten! It's certainly an improvement from the original, and I see you're now having it that the plants are being taken away by this "Mother"?

EDIT: I can also see that you took more of an opposite route for the "Mother" and "Son"; while the mother of the original was all in your face and the son was covered in animal features, the mother in this has almost no mention and the son has no features.

As for possible changes, you could try to reduce redundancy in that letter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not completely happy with it either - but I don't really know how I could make it less explain-y (not a word) without losing the reader.

I agree that it's too long and winded right now - I would likely repost this in like a month after I forget about this and come back with fresher ideas

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
1st one: https://www.flickr.com/photos/vivaopictures/3832590269/

2nd one: https://www.flickr.com/photos/tillwe/2195865084/

Both licenses indicate I am allowed to share and adapt with attribution

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This still needs a ton of work on the proofreading and structural fronts, let alone content. I noticed a "recorded recorded" in there (proofreading) and you had a sentence with two different howevers (structure). Those are just examples, there are many places where awkward sentence structure or hedging make even small bits of information take up way too much space.

Content-wise… the monster's abilities and intelligence level are ok, and the imagery of the rituals is ok. The containment method is an ok twist. All of this is marred by there being way too much text around any of it. This is especially true, as others above said, about the final note. There's maybe one paragraph of content worth mentioning in that whole note, and even that would probably work better if it's just implied. Instead, this breaks it way, way, way, way down, explaining it to the slightest detail. It isn't that complicated of a twist.

This needs more time in the oven. -1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

EDIT: Images are cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very valid point - I didn't see the 2845 connection earlier but it does seem to stray into that territory, which wasn't what I was aiming for.

I'll take that into consideration when I revamp this more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed, the end note is far too explainy, and this needs significant work in the structural department. There are far too many passages that don't quite work, tense is all over the place; see also "my naivete years".

The selected D-class personnel must not be given any plastic of metallic tools, but protective gloves and headgear are allowed. D-class personnel with physical disabilities should be allowed to join

Or this spot, with the 'of' and the sentence that just stops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me going for most of it, but the note at the end felt like a very pat way of summarizing the whole entry and ended up making it feel too much like SCP-2845.
